blockers: codex has required edits: Repair the shared lexer/classifier so regex-versus-division is decided from actual previous significant token context across all frozen expression-start contexts, including default-export and class-extends expressions, without treating keyword-named properties or methods as control/keyword contexts. Add classifier and closure regressions proving regex-contained load lookalikes create no edge and do not cause closure failure, while genuine adjacent loads remain visible.; Enforce b3 for every standard line-leading whitespace form admitted by the profile, not only space and tab, and add stable-diagnostic tests including form-feed or another non-space/tab ECMAScript whitespace character.; Complete b6 truncated-form detection, including a bare `import`, and add tests requiring `ProfileError` with diagnostic exactly `unsupported-module-lexical-profile`; audit the other accepted-form prefixes so none can return a successful no-edge classification when truncated.

codex has hard stops: AM-41 supported-profile correctness is not satisfied. The regex-versus-division logic is context-insensitive for required expression-start contexts. For example, `export default /import("\x2e\x2fghost.mjs")/;` is valid supported-profile source whose regex contains only a load lookalike, but `default` is absent from `REGEX_KEYWORDS`; the lexer treats the opening slash as division and `classifyModuleLoads` consequently infers a literal dynamic-import edge to `./ghost.mjs`. Closure derivation then falsely includes or fatally resolves that target. Similar defects exist around `extends` and keyword-named member calls because control-head and keyword decisions use only `prevSigWord`, without establishing token context.; AM-41's exact fail-closed set is incompletely enforced. A line-leading legacy close comment preceded by standard whitespace other than space/tab, such as form-feed (`\f-->`), is b3 and must throw `unsupported-module-lexical-profile`; the implementation checks only U+0020 and tab and accepts it. Truncated-form detection is also not total: a bare `import` is accepted as producing no sites rather than rejected as a truncated accepted module-load form under b6.

acceptance.note: Fail-closed: see gate.blockers and seat decisions.
